concept.name: Relational Model
concept.paragraph_1: The relational model organizes data into relations, commonly represented as tables composed of rows and columns.
concept.paragraph_2: Each row, called a tuple, represents an instance of an entity, while each column represents an attribute.
concept.paragraph_3: The model is grounded in set theory and first-order predicate logic, forming the theoretical foundation of relational algebra and SQL.

concept.name: Relational Model
concept.paragraph_1: The relational model organizes data into relations, commonly represented as tables composed of rows and columns.
concept.paragraph_2: Each row, called a tuple, represents an instance of an entity, while each column represents an attribute.
concept.paragraph_3: The model is grounded in set theory and first-order predicate logic, forming the theoretical foundation of relational algebra and SQL.

concept.name: ER Model
concept.paragraph_1: The Entity-Relationship (ER) model is a conceptual data model used to describe the logical structure of a database independently of physical implementation.
concept.paragraph_2: It represents entities as real-world objects, attributes as properties of those entities, and relationships as associations between entities.
concept.paragraph_3: ER diagrams are commonly used during the database design phase to transform conceptual schemas into relational schemas.

concept.name: ER Model
concept.paragraph_1: The Entity-Relationship (ER) model is a conceptual data model used to describe the logical structure of a database independently of physical implementation.
concept.paragraph_2: It represents entities as real-world objects, attributes as properties of those entities, and relationships as associations between entities.
concept.paragraph_3: ER diagrams are commonly used during the database design phase to transform conceptual schemas into relational schemas.

concept.paragraph_1: The Entity-Relationship (ER) model is a conceptual data model used to describe the logical structure of a database independently of physical implementation.
concept.paragraph_2: It represents entities as real-world objects, attributes as properties of those entities, and relationships as associations between entities.
concept.paragraph_3: ER diagrams are commonly used during the database design phase to transform conceptual schemas into relational schemas.

concept.paragraph_2: Each row, called a tuple, represents an instance of an entity, while each column represents an attribute.
concept.paragraph_3: The model is grounded in set theory and first-order predicate logic, forming the theoretical foundation of relational algebra and SQL.
